draft.introduction: Surah Al-An’am (The Cattle) is a Meccan surah focused on the unity of God, the folly of polytheism, and the signs of divine creation in nature. It contains some of the Quran’s most powerful arguments for monotheism.

translation.text: All praise is due to God, who created the heavens and the earth and made the darkness and the light. Yet those who disbelieve equate others with their Lord. It is He who created you from clay and then decreed a term. He knows what you conceal and what you reveal.

translator_notes.note: The pairing of darkness and light as divine creations asserts that both are under God’s sovereignty—a direct rejection of Zoroastrian and Manichaean dualism, which attributed darkness to an independent evil principle.
